title: Pharmacogenomic (PGx) gene–drug guidance
description: Baked pharmacogenomics (PGx) cross-check dataset (issue #710) — flags when a stored PGx result (a genomic_variants row, result_type='pharmacogenomic', #709) affects a medication in the profile's ACTIVE stack. CURATED HIGH-VALUE subset of the best-established CPIC/FDA gene–drug pairs, NOT exhaustive; the recommendation DIRECTION is PARAPHRASED (our words) and CITED to CPIC/FDA (all facts public/public-domain), the star-allele functions are public CPIC/PharmVar reference. INFORMATIONAL, never prescriptive — the absence of a flag is NOT clearance, and the app never auto-changes a medication. Fully OFFLINE: no gene/variant name ever leaves the box. Committed + HUMAN-REVIEWABLE; regenerate with `npm run gen:pgx`.

citation.source: CPIC (Clinical Pharmacogenetics Implementation Consortium) gene–drug guidelines; FDA drug labeling + Table of Pharmacogenetic Associations; RxNorm (NLM); PharmVar/CPIC allele-function tables.
citation.note: Recommendation DIRECTION paraphrased (uncopyrightable clinical fact) and cited per-row; allele functions are public reference data. Curated high-value subset, not exhaustive.

entries.guidance: clopidogrel activation is partly reduced, lowering its antiplatelet effect; CPIC recommends considering an alternative antiplatelet (e.g. prasugrel or ticagrelor), especially after PCI/ACS.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for clopidogrel and CYP2C19 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: clopidogrel is activated less, so its antiplatelet effect is reduced; CPIC recommends an alternative antiplatelet (e.g. prasugrel or ticagrelor) where clinically appropriate.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for clopidogrel and CYP2C19 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: increased metabolism lowers proton-pump-inhibitor exposure and may reduce efficacy; CPIC notes an increased dose may be warranted for the treatment goal.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for proton-pump inhibitors and CYP2C19 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: faster metabolism lowers proton-pump-inhibitor exposure and may reduce efficacy; CPIC notes an increased dose may be warranted for the treatment goal.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for proton-pump inhibitors and CYP2C19 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: reduced metabolism raises exposure to citalopram/escitalopram/sertraline; CPIC suggests a lower starting dose or an antidepressant not primarily metabolized by CYP2C19.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for SSRIs and CYP2D6/CYP2C19 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: faster metabolism lowers exposure and may reduce response; CPIC suggests considering an antidepressant not primarily metabolized by CYP2C19.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for SSRIs and CYP2D6/CYP2C19 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: reduced metabolism raises NSAID exposure and adverse-effect risk; CPIC recommends the lowest effective dose, a shorter course, or an NSAID not metabolized by CYP2C9.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for NSAIDs and CYP2C9 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: somewhat reduced clearance can raise phenytoin levels; CPIC recommends a reduced dose with therapeutic-level monitoring.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for phenytoin and CYP2C9/HLA-B (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: reduced clearance raises phenytoin levels and toxicity risk; CPIC recommends a reduced dose with therapeutic-level monitoring.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for phenytoin and CYP2C9/HLA-B (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: somewhat reduced clearance lowers the warfarin dose requirement; CPIC/validated dosing algorithms recommend a genotype-guided dose with INR monitoring.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for warfarin and CYP2C9/VKORC1 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: reduced clearance lowers the warfarin dose requirement; CPIC/validated dosing algorithms recommend a lower dose with close INR monitoring.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for warfarin and CYP2C9/VKORC1 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: little codeine is converted to morphine, so analgesia is likely inadequate; CPIC recommends an alternative analgesic (not tramadol).
entries.source: CPIC guideline for codeine/tramadol and CYP2D6 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: codeine is converted to morphine faster, risking toxicity; CPIC recommends AVOIDING codeine and using a non-CYP2D6 analgesic.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for codeine/tramadol and CYP2D6 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: reduced metabolism raises paroxetine/fluvoxamine exposure; CPIC suggests a lower dose or an SSRI not primarily metabolized by CYP2D6.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for SSRIs and CYP2D6/CYP2C19 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: endoxifen formation is somewhat reduced; CPIC notes lower exposure and suggests weighing an alternative where clinically appropriate.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for tamoxifen and CYP2D6 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: less active endoxifen is formed, which may lower efficacy; CPIC recommends considering an alternative (e.g. an aromatase inhibitor) where clinically appropriate.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for tamoxifen and CYP2D6 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: reduced metabolism raises tricyclic exposure; CPIC suggests a ~50% lower dose with monitoring, or an alternative.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for tricyclic antidepressants and CYP2D6/CYP2C19 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: faster metabolism lowers tricyclic exposure; CPIC suggests an alternative not metabolized by CYP2D6, or therapeutic-level monitoring.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for tricyclic antidepressants and CYP2D6/CYP2C19 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: less active metabolite is formed, so analgesia is likely reduced; CPIC recommends an alternative analgesic (not codeine).
entries.source: CPIC guideline for codeine/tramadol and CYP2D6 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: more active metabolite is formed, risking toxicity; CPIC recommends AVOIDING tramadol and using a non-CYP2D6 analgesic.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for codeine/tramadol and CYP2D6 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: reduced clearance raises toxicity risk; CPIC recommends a reduced starting dose (about 50%) with titration to tolerance.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for fluoropyrimidines and DPYD (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: greatly reduced clearance risks severe or fatal toxicity; CPIC recommends AVOIDING 5-fluorouracil/capecitabine and selecting an alternative.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for fluoropyrimidines and DPYD (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: HLA-A*31:01 positivity raises the risk of carbamazepine hypersensitivity reactions (including SJS/TEN and DRESS); CPIC recommends considering an alternative.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for carbamazepine/oxcarbazepine and HLA-B/HLA-A (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: a positive HLA-B*57:01 result marks high risk of a serious abacavir hypersensitivity reaction; CPIC and FDA labeling recommend NOT using abacavir.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for abacavir and HLA-B (cpicpgx.org); FDA boxed warning

entries.guidance: HLA-B*15:02 positivity marks high risk of Stevens–Johnson syndrome / toxic epidermal necrolysis; CPIC recommends AVOIDING carbamazepine in carbamazepine-naïve patients.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for carbamazepine/oxcarbazepine and HLA-B/HLA-A (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: HLA-B*15:02 positivity marks high risk of Stevens–Johnson syndrome / toxic epidermal necrolysis; CPIC recommends AVOIDING oxcarbazepine in oxcarbazepine-naïve patients.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for carbamazepine/oxcarbazepine and HLA-B/HLA-A (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: HLA-B*15:02 positivity raises the risk of Stevens–Johnson syndrome / toxic epidermal necrolysis; CPIC recommends AVOIDING phenytoin if an alternative is available.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for phenytoin and CYP2C9/HLA-B (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: NUDT15 activity is reduced; CPIC recommends a reduced starting dose with monitoring.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for thiopurines and TPMT/NUDT15 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: reduced NUDT15 activity raises the risk of severe myelosuppression at standard doses; CPIC recommends a substantially reduced dose or an alternative.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for thiopurines and TPMT/NUDT15 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: decreased transporter function moderately raises simvastatin exposure/myopathy risk; CPIC recommends a lower dose or an alternative statin.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for statins and SLCO1B1 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: reduced hepatic uptake raises simvastatin exposure and myopathy risk; CPIC recommends a lower dose or an alternative statin.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for statins and SLCO1B1 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: thiopurine inactivation is reduced; CPIC recommends a reduced starting dose with monitoring.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for thiopurines and TPMT/NUDT15 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: very little thiopurine is inactivated, so standard doses risk severe myelosuppression; CPIC recommends a substantially reduced dose or an alternative.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for thiopurines and TPMT/NUDT15 (cpicpgx.org)

entries.guidance: the VKORC1 −1639 A allele increases warfarin sensitivity (a lower dose requirement); CPIC recommends genotype-guided dosing with INR monitoring.
entries.source: CPIC guideline for warfarin and CYP2C9/VKORC1 (cpicpgx.org)
